attacking_style: Early-race aggressor who launches multiple attacks to initiate or join breakaways, often targeting intermediate sprints and willing to make repeated efforts before successful moves establish.
team_tactics: Functions as breakaway instigator for B&B Hotels, given freedom to animate races through solo attacks rather than supported by team tempo control.
terrain_preference: Suits races where breakaway formation is tactically viable; appears comfortable in rolling terrain allowing repeated acceleration efforts rather than pure climbing or flat stages.
vulnerability: Attacks tend to be numerous but ineffective when peloton is unwilling to grant freedom; lacks finishing power to convert breakaway participation into results without selective terrain.
key_patterns: Launches multiple attacks per race (3+ attempts documented) seeking breakaway placement.
Targets intermediate sprint points as tactical objectives during breakaway efforts.
Acts as breakaway catalyst/initiator rather than counter-attacker or selective aggressor.
form_trajectory: unknown: 3-year gap between observations prevents meaningful trajectory assessment, though role consistency suggests stable capabilities
